strengths: Clearly identifies three specific objects with real-world contexts
Explicitly links each object to the chosen IA prompt
Demonstrates personal engagement, grounding objects in lived experience
weaknesses: Analysis is largely descriptive and repetitive with limited concrete evidence
TOK concepts (Ways of Knowing, Areas of Knowledge) are not explicitly integrated
Metaphorical treatment of the prism is loosely connected and lacks depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student meets the descriptor for the top of the 3â4 band by presenting three well-contextualized objects, each linked explicitly to IA prompt 34. Justifications for inclusion are offered and reference the prompt, but the discussion remains largely assertive, thinly evidenced and repetitive. TOK terminology and concrete examples are missing or underdeveloped, and the prism metaphor is not firmly rooted in real-world analysis. While the work goes beyond minimal links, it falls short of the precision, depth and evidence required for a 5â6 band. A score of 4 accurately reflects its adequate yet limited effectiveness, in line with the official examinerâs total mark.

annotations.comment: The introduction names the chosen prompt but presumes shared understanding of âvaleursâ and âacquisition des connaissances.â Define these key terms explicitly to anchor your analysis in TOK terminology.
How does the student interpret âacquisition des connaissancesâ in a TOK framework (e.g., through Ways of Knowing or Areas of Knowledge)? Clarification would strengthen the conceptual foundation.
The studentâs personal engagement with the Qurâan is clear, grounding the object in a real lived experience and illustrating why it was selected.
The analysis of the Qurâan relies on broad assertions about its ethical influence but omits explicit TOK concepts such as Ways of Knowing or bias, which would deepen the link to the prompt.
Several paragraphs describe general influence of the Qurâan without concrete examples (e.g., specific teachings or case studies). Include at least one detailed example to support your claims.
To deepen analysis, integrate TOK theory such as how ethical values shape the selection of Ways of Knowing, showing a clear bridge to the prompt about values and knowledge acquisition.
The discussion repeats the same assertion about values influencing conclusions. Focus on one novel insight per paragraph and eliminate redundancy to maintain precision.
This description of the MUN context remains largely narrative; explicitly link how Qatarâs values shaped your research choices in TOK terms.
Provide a caption and provenance for the badge image to clarify its authenticity and enhance the real-world context.
Assertions about bias in source selection require concrete examples of sources or statements you used to demonstrate how values steered your research.
Reference specific MUN position papers or country statements to ground your claim about Qatarâs value-driven selection of sources.
How does this MUN example move beyond description to illustrate the nuances of knowledge acquisition in accordance with the prompt?
The word-count note indicates 334 words here; ensure balanced allocation across objects so each receives sufficient analytical depth.
The prismâs physical properties are described clearly, presenting a tangible real-world object.
Specify when and where you obtained or used the prism to anchor its relevance in a specific real-world context.
The prism metaphor is promising, but its link to TOK concepts is only implied. Explicitly connect it to ways of knowing or perception to solidify the comparison.
Tie the prism analogy to a concrete situation (e.g., peer review, cultural interpretation) where values filter information in practice.
Consider citing specific verses or chapters from the Qurâan to support your claims, providing concrete evidence rather than broad statements.
Monitor word count allocation: 335 words here leave limited space for deeper analysis. Redistribute words to balance description with critical reflection.
The object title is clear, but adding context (e.g., edition, year received) here would strengthen specificity of the real-world setting.
The MUN badge example effectively roots the object in a concrete personal experience, illustrating real-world context.
The claim that values filter information cognitively is asserted broadly; explain the cognitive mechanism or reference a TOK expert to substantiate this claim.
Incorporate TOK vocabulary (e.g., perception, emotion, interpretation) here to show how values influence specific Ways of Knowing.
The conclusion restates earlier points without addressing possible limitations of the prism metaphor or alternative perspectives.
Offer an alternative metaphor or counterexample to demonstrate critical engagement and show awareness of the metaphorâs limitations.
Review the 268-word count here to ensure sufficient space remains for a critical evaluation of the metaphorâs efficacy.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
